title: Digital Connection vs. Mental Well-being
content: Title slide introducing a presentation on the relationship between digital connection and mental well-being. Presented by Li Wei, a Year 2 Sociology student, for Global Studies 201.

title: The Ubiquitous Landscape
content: This slide describes Gen Z as Digital Natives who experience life through an online lens, supported by usage statistics for YouTube, Instagram, and Snapchat. It emphasizes that constant connectivity has become an indispensable part of modern life.
key_claims: Gen Z are recognized as Digital Natives, experiencing life through an online lens
70% of students engage with Instagram and Snapchat
Constant connectivity is a fundamental and indispensable reality in the modern era

title: Usage and Anxiety Statistics
content: This slide presents a bar chart comparing social media usage levels with reported anxiety and depression risk among users. Data is sourced from the Mental Health Statistics Report 2025.
key_claims: 87% of users have usage exceeding 2 hours/day

title: Psychological Manifestations
content: This slide outlines three key psychological effects of social media use: FOMO, social comparison, and cyberbullying. Each is presented with an icon and brief explanation of its impact on mental health.
key_claims: FOMO Effect: fear of missing out creates significant stress and anxiety regarding social inclusion
Comparison: constant utilization of curated feeds leads to negative self-image and low self-esteem issues
Cyberbullying: nearly 87% of teens have experienced some form of online harassment or negative interactions

title: Strategic Mitigation Efforts
content: This slide proposes three strategies to address the negative mental health impacts of digital connection: digital detox, awareness of platform algorithms, and platform responsibility. It calls for both individual and corporate action.
key_claims: Digital Detox: implementing time limits is a crucial strategy for emotional balance
Awareness: education regarding platform algorithms is essentially significant
Platform Responsibility: companies should prioritize user well-being as a primary imperative
